state_manifold.embedding_function: Identity embedding of bitvector; energy defines ordering for descent
constraints: Time is discrete path length (# generator moves)

generators.name: flip
generators.transition_rule: x → x' where x'_i = 1 - x_i for specified index i

generators.name: flip_if_one
generators.transition_rule: x → x' where x'_i = 0 if x_i = 1; otherwise fails
generators.invariant_effect: Guarded flip; exposes MOVE_PRECONDITION_FAILED obstruction surface

invariants.name: BIT_DOMAIN
invariants.definition: States are bitvectors of length N with entries in {0,1}

failure_taxonomy.trigger_condition: schema_version mismatch or JSON schema violation
bitvector length != N, values not in {0,1}, or generator index i not in [0..N-1]
attempted_moves or chosen_move includes a move not listed in generator_set; membership is defined on the pair (name,i)
move-specific preconditions fail (e.g., flip_if_one requires bit i == 1)
chosen_move is not greedy argmin over attempted legal moves (tie-break by move key name:i)
a probe expects reachable=true but BFS cannot reach goal within k under declared generator_set

reachability.component_analysis: Legal transitions form a directed graph on {0,1}^N. Optional return_in_k_probes are verified by BFS up to depth k using the declared generator_set (order-preserving compilation, identical moves deduplicated).

determinism_contract.nondeterminism_proof: Move application is deterministic. Energy is deterministic (min Hamming distance to any memory). Greedy policy is deterministic via ordering (energy_after, move_sort_key). Probes are deterministic via BFS with fixed generator order.
